zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret FitzAlan於1370年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，她的父親Sir John FitzAlan20歲，母親Eleanor Mautravers25歲。她於1394年10月9日與Sir William de Ros, Knt.在英國萊希斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1438年7月在英國諾薩普頓史爾享年68歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret FitzAlan was born in 1370, in Arundel, Sussex, England, her father, Sir John FitzAlan, was 20 and her mother, Eleanor Mautravers, was 25. She married Sir William de Ros, Knt. on 9 October 1394, in Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died in July 1438, in Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in Northamptonshire, England.

Elle a épousé Sir William de Ros, Knt. le 9 octobre 1394, à Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en juillet 1438, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret FitzAlan(이)가 1370년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John FitzAlan(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Mautravers(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1394년 10월 9일 영국 레이스스터실르에서 Sir William de Ros, Knt.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1438년 7월 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret FitzAlan родилась в 1370 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John FitzAlan было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Mautravers, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Ros, Knt. 9 октября 1394 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла июль 1438 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности нортамптоншир, Англия.
